attacking_style: Solo attacks from breakaways on HC mountain finishes, capable of dropping quality climbers in final kilometers. Demonstrates ability to win consecutive mountain stages when in form.
team_tactics: Bahrain allows freedom in breakaways for stage hunting rather than pure GC protection. Teammates like PORT provide diversionary late attacks for time bonuses while PADUN targets stage wins.
terrain_preference: HC category mountain finishes where pure climbing power decides outcomes. Excels on sustained final climbs where he can grind down rivals in closing kilometers.
vulnerability: Equipment failures at critical moments (broken wheel cost stage win at Giro). Late-race fatigue may limit ability to recover from mechanical setbacks when already at threshold.
key_patterns: Wins from breakaways on consecutive HC mountain stages, demonstrating repeatable stage-hunting capability (2 stages)
Operates dual objectives: stage wins plus KOM jersey calculations simultaneously
Outclimbs established WorldTour climbers (Kuss) on HC finishes when in peak form
Mechanical issues compound when already fatigued, suggesting limited margin for error in Grand Tours
form_trajectory: positive: Back-to-back HC mountain stage wins at age 24 showing emergence as genuine WorldTour-level climber and stage hunter
